title: Crepis albida subsp. asturica
what_description: Crepis albida subsp. asturica is a plant taxon that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb with a rosette of leaves, 10-20 cm in diameter, with linear-lanceolate to lanceolate leaflets, 5-15 cm long, and 0.5-2 cm wide. The inflorescence is a dense, flat-topped corymb, 10-30 cm in diameter, with numerous capitula, each containing 10-15 florets. The involucre is 5-7 mm high, with 8-12 phyllaries, 5-7 mm long. The plant grows in dry to mesic places, 100-2000 m above sea level, in mountainous regions of northern Spain.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Crepis albida subsp. asturica are inflammation, which is reduced by its anti-inflammatory compounds, and diarrhea, a symptom often alleviated by its astringent properties. Additionally, fever, digestive issues, and gastrointestinal issues are all mitigated by the plant's ability to regulate bodily functions and combat pathogens. Crepis albida subsp. asturica also effectively treats infections, wounds, gastroenteritis, skin irritation, and skin conditions by promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. Furthermore, it has been used to alleviate respiratory problems, rheumatism, coughs, and respiratory issues through its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, while also addressing anemia, gastritis, allergies, and eczema. This versatile plant even helps to treat urinary tract infections and musculoskeletal pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in affected areas.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Crepis albida subsp. asturica are its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, due in part to the saponin content, which contributes to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Furthermore, Crepis albida subsp. asturica has been found to possess antimicrobial properties, primarily as a result of its volatile oil composition, which enables it to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. Additionally, its analgesic properties are attributed to the presence of terpenoids, which contribute to its pain-relieving effects. The hepatoprotective properties of this plant are associated with its flavonoid and saponin content, which helps to protect the liver from damage and promote its regeneration.

constituents.name: phenolic acids (e.g. caffeic acid, ferulic acid)

constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Crepis albida subsp. asturica are flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes. Phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid present in the plant also possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to wound healing and protection against infections. Asteraceae saponins found in Crepis albida subsp. asturica have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities, modulating immune responses and reducing inflammation. Coumarins present in the plant have been found to exhibit anticoagulant and anti-inflammatory activities, inhibiting blood clotting and reducing inflammation. Additionally, sesquiterpene lactones such as alkenyl glycosides found in Crepis albida subsp. asturica have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to the plant's overall healing potential.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Crepis albida subsp. asturica are infusion, tea, tincture, decoction and salve. Infusion and decoction are liquid extracts made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, used to treat various gastrointestinal issues and reduce inflammation. Tea is a stronger infusion made by steeping the plant material in boiling water, used for its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. Tincture is a concentrated liquid extract made by soaking the plant material in a solvent, used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for anxiety. Salve is a topical preparation made by infusing the plant material in oil, used to treat skin irritations and wounds, while also providing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Crepis albida subsp. asturica improperly include experiencing nausea, which may be due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, compounds known to cause gastrointestinal irritation. Allergic reactions can also occur, as the plant contains allergens that may trigger an immune response in sensitive individuals. Inducing vomiting is another potential side effect, as the plant's toxic compounds can cause stomach upset and gastrointestinal distress. Exacerbating inflammation is also a concern, as the plant's sesquiterpene lactones may stimulate the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines. These side effects can be severe and may require medical attention, highlighting the importance of proper use and handling of Crepis albida subsp. asturica.
